title: model-gear now serves an embedding gear (Qwen3-Embedding-4B) and a reranker gear (Qwen3-Reranker) as always-warm fleet backends co-resident with the Qwen3.6-27B primary on the shared DGX Spark — every consumer reaches all three through one OpenAI-compatible gateway, routed by the request's model field.

claims.text: model-gear now serves an embedding gear (Qwen3-Embedding-4B) and a reranker gear (Qwen3-Reranker) as always-warm fleet backends co-resident with the Qwen3.6-27B primary on the shared DGX Spark — every consumer reaches all three through one OpenAI-compatible gateway, routed by the request's model field.

honesty_conditions.text: All three backends (27B chat, Qwen3-Embedding-4B, Qwen3-Reranker) are simultaneously healthy on the shared GB10, and one gateway port serves chat + /v1/embeddings + /v1/rerank, each routed by the request's model field.

claims.text: jetson-ai-lab-cli (primary; filed #44, building its Discord+docs index) and eidetic-cli (storage+retrieval, second consumer); both call it as an ordinary OpenAI-compatible provider — base_url + api_key + model name, no model-gear-specific SDK.

an indexing client POSTs /v1/embeddings {model,input:[...]} and gets vectors, and POSTs /v1/rerank {model,query,documents:[...]} and gets ranked scores — both served warm by model-gear through the same gateway port the chat primary already uses.
the gateway already proxies /v1/embeddings but nothing warm sits behind it; the catalog holds only generative chat gears; there is no reranker anywhere in model-gear.
this unblocks the indexing half of jetson-ai-lab-cli and eidetic's retrieval — model-gear vectorizes, eidetic stores and retrieves; the two compose cleanly.
model-gear only SERVES the gears (embeddings + scores over HTTP); it does NOT store vectors, build/own an index, chunk text, or do retrieval — that is eidetic's half. No vector DB lands here.

model overview --list shows the embedding + reranker gears (tagged load-tested/configured); curl /v1/embeddings returns a vector of the documented dimension; curl /v1/rerank returns ranked scores; per-model docs record dimension + throughput + co-residency; model explain embeddings/rerank/score document the call shapes.

Topology: the embedding + reranker run as ADDITIONAL always-warm fleet backends (extra vllm services + gateway routing by model name), not via single-model swaps. model switch also gains embed/score (--task) support so an embed gear can be served solo for isolated load-testing and to satisfy the curl acceptance test.

Models: Qwen3-Embedding-4B served at native 2560-dim (MRL-truncatable down to 32 via hf-overrides/dimensions); reranker is Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B (smallest — leaves the most 27B headroom), with Qwen3-Reranker-4B documented as an upgrade path.

hard_questions.text: risk: Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B is a CausalLM needing the Qwen3ForSequenceClassification hf-override to use the efficient score API; if the override fails on the pinned nv26.04 image the reranker stays configured-not-load-tested (acceptable — issue #44's second priority).

claims.text: The catalog SupportedModel must express task (generate|embed|score), embedding dimension, and per-model hf-overrides / vLLM serve-extras, so embed and score gears are first-class catalog rows alongside chat gears (and the wheel-shipped catalog stays the single source of truth).

honesty_conditions.text: model overview --list and /v1/models/supported show the embed+score gears with their task + dimension; tests assert catalog<->compose-template<->per-model-doc consistency for the new task/dim/hf_overrides fields (mirroring the existing test_catalog invariants).

claims.text: The gateway must route embedding + score/rerank requests to the right backend by model name and list the warm embed+score backends in /v1/models; embed/score/rerank bodies all carry a model field, so handle_post's existing model-routing covers them — verify, do not assume.

honesty_conditions.text: with the fleet up, curl /v1/embeddings {model:<embed>,input:[hi]} returns data[0].embedding of the documented length AND curl /v1/chat/completions still answers from the 27B — both warm, same gateway; any handle_post change is shown necessary by a failing case, not assumed.

claims.text: model explain must document the embeddings, rerank, and score call shapes (request + response + dimension + served model names) so a consumer wires it from the docs alone; model overview --list and /v1/models/supported must surface the new gears.

honesty_conditions.text: a consumer wires the integration using only 'model explain embeddings' + 'model explain rerank' (request+response+dimension+served names) with no other reference; the documented served names equal the catalog ids.

claims.text: Not adding a vector database, not changing any consumer's code, not building a generic multi-task autoscaler, not removing or demoting the 27B chat primary.

The embedder (~4B) + reranker (~0.6B) co-fit alongside the 27B primary (~70GB at util 0.6) within the GB10's 128GB unified memory; lowering the primary's gpu-mem-util to make room is acceptable headroom if measurement shows it is needed.

open_vagueness.text: Whether Qwen3-Reranker loads under the Qwen3ForSequenceClassification hf-override on the pinned nv26.04 vLLM image (its score-converter may postdate that build); the reranker ships 'configured' until proven on the box. The embedding gear (the primary ask) does not depend on it.
